stories.headline: Iran war live: Trump reviews peace plan; UN calls for Hormuz to reopen
stories.summary: UN chief says US-Iran standoff in the Strait of Hormuz risks triggering a global food emergency.

stories.headline: First LNG Shipment Since Iran War Began Appears to Exit Hormuz
stories.summary: The first liquefied natural gas shipment since the war in the Middle East began two months ago appears to have traversed the Strait of Hormuz to exit the Persian Gulf.

stories.headline: Trump Discusses Latest Iran Proposal With Aides
stories.summary: The White House has said US officials were discussing Iran’s latest proposal but maintained “red lines” on any deal to end the eight-week war, including preventing Tehran from obtaining a nuclear weapon. Bloomberg's Laura Davison reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: White House to review security in light of shooting at correspondents’ dinner
stories.summary: “If adjustments need to be made to protect the president, they will be made,” White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt told reporters Monday.

stories.headline: What we're learning about suspected Washington press dinner gunman
stories.summary: The man arrested at the event attended by President Trump appeared in court on Monday.

Korea Passes UK to Become World’s Eighth-Largest Stock Market
stories.summary: South Korea has leapfrogged the UK to become the world’s eighth-biggest stock market, fueled by a high-octane rally in its artificial intelligence-linked technology champions.

stories.headline: Nissan Shares Climb After Forecasting Profit Instead of Loss
stories.summary: Nissan Motor Co. shares climbed the most in more than two months after the Japanese carmaker raised its earnings outlook, avoiding what could have been its first annual operating loss in five years.

stories.headline: Ukraine summons Israel's ambassador over allegedly stolen grain being shipped to Haifa
stories.summary: Kyiv has warned Israel of a brewing diplomatic crisis, should authorities permit a vessel carrying grain allegedly stolen from Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories to dock and unload.

stories.headline: King Charles’s rare state visit offers U.K. a chance to mend ties with Trump

stories.headline: Israeli settlers attack Palestinians and set house on fire

stories.headline: Thousands held by paramilitary RSF in Sudan’s el-Fasher: NGO

stories.headline: Merz suggests Ukraine’s EU membership may depend on territorial concessions
stories.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on Monday that Ukraine’s proposed timeline for EU membership is unrealistic, as debate over enlargement continues to intensify. “Zelenskyy had the idea of joining the EU on January 1, 2027. That will not work. Even January 1, 2028, is not realistic."

stories.headline: Daiwa Buys Orix Bank for $2.3 Billion in Loans Push; Shares Fall
stories.summary: Daiwa Securities Group Inc. will buy Orix Corp.’s banking unit for ¥370 billion ($2.3 billion), its largest acquisition in almost two decades, as Japan’s second-biggest brokerage deepens its foray into commercial lending.

stories.headline: Yield Outlook Makes Japan’s Life Insurers Go Slow on JGBs

stories.headline: EU faces ‘China shock’ as EV imports drive Beijing’s record surplus with bloc
stories.summary: China sold goods worth about $148bn to EU in first quarter of year, but imported just $65bnThe EU is experiencing a prolonged “China shock” as a flood of Chinese EVs into Europe helped push Beijing to a record surplus with the bloc.New data showed China’s trade surplus – where its exports to the EU exceeded imports from the bloc – was $83bn (£61bn) in the first three months of 2026. stories.headline: Woman trapped in poo for three hours after outback toilet collapses

stories.headline: Why is this game only legal across Australia one day a year?
